inline_comments.message:
Just to make sure if you dump the "json" string here with a System.out.println(), what does it look like?   

Clients won't be using StandardCoder for decoding the string.
it should be fine, but add another assert to get the plainString to be complete (or remove from 244.

this change doesn't impact encoding, per se: if you put an integer into a map and encode it, it will not have a decimal point in the resulting json, while a double WILL have a decimal point.  Thus, 10 is encoded as "10" and 10D as "10.0".

When decoding either of those GSON, will convert them both to 10D, by default.  The result is, if you turn around and encode this back into a json string, then it will always be "10.0".  With this change, when the string is decoded, "10.0" will be converted to 10, while 10.5 will be converted to 10.5D.  Consequently, if it's encoded back into json, it will look the same as the original string.

BTW: the json looks like:
{"props":{"plainString":"def","doubleVal":12.5,"negInt":-10,"posLong":100000000000}}
